title: convolvulus demissus var. ovatus
intro: Convolvulus demissus var. ovatus, also known as the small bindweed or dwarf morning glory, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Convolvulus demissus var. ovatus include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and can be used to create teas, salves, and creams that can be used to treat a range of health issues.
